packet.claim_text: A discovery-memory packet is safer when provenance, privacy boundary, measurable variables, and falsification route are captured before backlog admission.
missingness: No external evidence source attached yet
packet.revision_reason: Create a minimal provenance unit for routing claims through MC evidence gates.

measurable_variables.name: required_label_completion_rate
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Fraction of required provenance labels present and non-empty before packet admission.

measurable_variables.name: blocked_use_count
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Number of explicitly blocked use categories recorded in the evidence boundary.

packet.falsification_route: If packets with missing provenance labels or private-use leakage pass validation, the schema and validator are insufficient.
packet.next_executable_action: Run the validator on synthetic fixtures and add it to the ingestion path.

packet.claim_text: This packet should fail because it asks to store private personal detail in discovery memory.

measurable_variables.name: privacy_leak_flag
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Binary flag indicating whether the packet attempts to retain private or identifying content.

packet.falsification_route: If this packet passes admission, the privacy gate is not strict enough.
packet.next_executable_action: Reject and request a public-safe abstraction.

packet.claim_text: This packet should fail because the falsification route is too vague for scientific routing.

measurable_variables.name: falsification_specificity
measurable_variables.operational_definition: Text score for whether the falsification route identifies a concrete failure condition.
